title: centrolepis cephaloformis subsp. murrayi
intro: Centrolepis cephaloformis subsp. murrayi, also known as Murray's needlewort, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Centrolepidaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including the potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antioxidant activity. It is often prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
